channel.title: Nature Communications
channel.description:
Nature Communications is an open access, multidisciplinary journal dedicated to publishing high-quality research in all areas of the biological, health, physical, chemical and Earth sciences. Papers published by the journal aim to represent important advances of significance to specialists within each field.

We are committed to providing an efficient service for both authors and readers. Our team of independent editors make rapid and fair publication decisions. Prompt dissemination of accepted papers to a wide readership and beyond is achieved through a programme of continuous online publication.
channel.dc:rights: © 2026 Macmillan Publishers Limited, part of Springer Nature. All rights reserved.
channel.prism:copyright: © 2026 Macmillan Publishers Limited, part of Springer Nature. All rights reserved.

item.title: Metasurface-enhanced mid-infrared imaging spectroscopy with broadband quantum cascade lasers
item.content:encoded:
Nature Communications, Published online: 07 April 2026; doi:10.1038/s41467-026-71611-9

Authors present an imaging-based mid-IR spectroscopy platform using resonance gradient metasurfaces and a broadband quantum cascade laser. Enhanced molecular absorption is captured in a single frame of an imaging detector for rapid, low-cost detection.
item.dc:title: Metasurface-enhanced mid-infrared imaging spectroscopy with broadband quantum cascade lasers
item.dc:creator: Ivan Sinev
Alessio Cargioli
Diego Piciocchi
Felix Ulrich Brikh
Jérôme Faist
Hatice Altug

item.title: Regulating interface electric field to stabilize high-voltage KVPO<sub>4</sub>F positive electrode for sustainable potassium-ion batteries
item.content:encoded:
Nature Communications, Published online: 07 April 2026; doi:10.1038/s41467-026-71647-x

Potassium-ion batteries face stability issues at high voltages. Here, authors use nitrogen-doped nanotubes to regulate the interfacial electric field of a polyanionic KVPO4F positive electrode. This stabilizes the structure and interface, enabling a specific energy of 454.8 Wh kg−1 and a cycling lifespan over 2000 cycles.
item.dc:title: Regulating interface electric field to stabilize high-voltage KVPO<sub>4</sub>F positive electrode for sustainable potassium-ion batteries
item.dc:creator: Haijie Qi
Yichen Du
Jianfeng Ding
Lili Song
Jiaying Liao
Congying Yu
Zeyu Yuan
Shiang Chen
Xiaosi Zhou

item.title: Intrinsic attraction driving the high temperature performance of additively manufactured aluminum alloys
item.content:encoded:
Nature Communications, Published online: 07 April 2026; doi:10.1038/s41467-026-71390-3

This study introduces an intrinsic attraction strategy to enhance the high-temperature performance of additively manufactured aluminum alloys. By confining atomic defects and stabilizing precipitates, the alloy retains 305 MPa at 300 °C with creep resistance up to 400 °C.
item.dc:title: Intrinsic attraction driving the high temperature performance of additively manufactured aluminum alloys
item.dc:creator: Yueting Wang
Chengzhe Yu
Kefu Gan
Tiechui Yuan
Ruidi Li

item.title: Unveiling the synergistic mechanism of C-F and C-Cl bonds in enhancing the triboelectric performance of fluorinated polymers
item.content:encoded:
Nature Communications, Published online: 07 April 2026; doi:10.1038/s41467-026-71546-1

The enhancement of tribo-negative performance in traditional fluorinated polymers is important for the design of triboelectric devices. Here, the authors describe the enhancements of the tribo-negative properties of fluorinated copolymers via the synergistic effects of C-F and C-Cl bonds in the polymers.
item.dc:title: Unveiling the synergistic mechanism of C-F and C-Cl bonds in enhancing the triboelectric performance of fluorinated polymers
item.dc:creator: Jinmei Liu
Fanyu Zhang
Jiongyao Xu
Shengyang Jia
Nuanyang Cui
Rusen Yang
Weiwei Wang
Long Gu
